name: George Duke
abstract: George Martin Duke (January 12, 1946 – August 5, 2013) was an American keyboardist, composer, singer-songwriter and record producer. He worked with numerous artists as arranger, music director, writer and co-writer, record producer and as a professor of music.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Cannonball Adderley Quintet, Oscar Peterson, and Mahavishnu Orchestra

about_quotes.speaker: David Torkanowsky
about_quotes.evidence: I used to play second keyboards for a time behind George Duke.
about_quotes.doc_title: David Torkanowsky interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, New York, September 26, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Ramsey Lewis
about_quotes.evidence: She had a morning show. As I recall, she was on a station that played George Duke, Wes Montgomery, all the great players, before they brought in smooth jazz.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ramsey Lewis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Scott Yanow
about_quotes.evidence: I listened to some Stan Kenton ballad albums... And then a George Duke album that actually has him playing jazz in the early 70s.
about_quotes.doc_title: Scott Yanow interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, October 22, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Onaje Allan Gumbs
about_quotes.evidence: And it stayed with me, as it has stayed with people like George Duke, who went through Cannonball’s band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Onaje Allan Gumbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Malone
about_quotes.evidence: he calls to say I had the job playing with George Duke and Jean Luc Ponty on the tour in ’73.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Malone interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 3, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Roy McCurdy
about_quotes.evidence: And then George Duke came up with a tune in five-four that we did
about_quotes.doc_title: Roy McCurdy interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, November 16, 1995
